endpoints.description: Any of the 27 available Bitcoin charts by name. Use /charts catalog to see all available types. Chart types: market-price, market-cap, trade-volume, total-bitcoins, n-transactions, n-unique-addresses, hash-rate, difficulty, miners-revenue, transaction-fees, mempool-size, mempool-count, utxo-count, avg-block-size, median-confirmation-time, and more.

Catalog of all 27 Bitcoin chart types organized into 9 groups: market (price, cap, volume), transactions (daily, total, per block), addresses (unique, wallet users), volume (output, estimated), mining (hashrate, difficulty, revenue, fees), blocks (size, orphaned), mempool (size, count, growth), network (UTXO, confirmation times), supply (total BTC). Use chart names with /chart/{type} endpoint.
Bitcoin address activity over time: unique addresses used per day (proxy for network adoption), Blockchain.com wallet user count.
Bitcoin block data over time: average block size, total blockchain size, orphaned (stale) blocks. Blockchain growth rate indicator.
Bitcoin market data over time: market-price (USD), market-cap, trade-volume. Timespans: 7days, 30days, 60days, 180days, 1year, 2years, all.
Bitcoin mempool over time: size in bytes, transaction count, growth rate. Historical view of network congestion patterns.
Bitcoin mining metrics over time: hashrate (TH/s), difficulty, miner revenue (BTC), total transaction fees, cost per transaction. Shows network security level and miner economics.
Bitcoin network health over time: UTXO set size (unspent outputs - measures state bloat), median and average confirmation times (measures congestion and user experience).
Total Bitcoin in circulation over time. Approaching the 21 million hard cap. Block reward halves every ~210,000 blocks (~4 years). Current supply ~20M BTC, last BTC mined ~2140.
Bitcoin transaction activity over time: confirmed transactions per day, cumulative total, transactions per block, transactions excluding popular addresses. Key indicator of network usage.
Bitcoin transaction volumes over time: total output volume (BTC), estimated transaction volume in BTC and USD. Shows economic throughput of the Bitcoin network.
Most recent Bitcoin block - hash, height, timestamp, block index.
Pending Bitcoin transactions in the mempool. Returns up to 50 most recent unconfirmed transactions with hash, size, fee, input/output counts.
Bitcoin mining pools ranked by blocks found in the given timespan. Shows pool name, block count, and share percentage. Timespans: 7days, 30days, 60days, 180days, 1year, 2years, all.

Comprehensive Bitcoin network stats in one call - 20+ metrics: market price (USD), hashrate, difficulty, transactions per day, blocks mined, minutes between blocks, total BTC in circulation, estimated transaction volume (USD), miner revenue, trade volume, total fees, next retarget block, block size. Source: Blockchain.com.
Current Bitcoin price in 30 fiat currencies with buy/sell/last prices. USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, CAD, CHF, BRL, ARS, and 21 more. Updates every 15 minutes.

Per-bank retail lending benchmarks curated by Bank Negara Malaysia for all licensed conventional and Islamic banks operating in Malaysia (35 institutions). Returns Base Rate (BR), Base Lending Rate (BLR, conventional) or Base Financing Rate (BFR, Islamic), and the Indicative Effective Lending Rate for a standard reference housing loan.
endpoints.summary: BNM per-bank Base Rate, BLR, BFR, Indicative ELR

endpoints.description: BNM MYR exchange-rate fixing across 27 currencies. Four daily sessions: `0900` Interbank Intervention Rate (USD-only pair), `1130` Best Counter Rate (majors only), `1200` Reference Rate (27 currencies), `1700` Reference Rate closing (default, 27 currencies). The `quote` param toggles quote direction: `rm` (default, MYR per unit foreign currency) or `fc` (foreign currency per MYR). Units vary per currency (1 for major currencies, 100 for minor / high-denomination currencies such as JPY, IDR, VND, KHR, NPR); the response exposes both the raw per-unit rate and a normalised `per_unit_*` trio.

parameters.description: Optional session identifier. One of `0900`, `1130`, `1200`, `1700`. Default upstream: `1700`.
parameters.name: session

schema.description: Optional session identifier. One of `0900`, `1130`, `1200`, `1700`. Default upstream: `1700`.
schema.title: Session

parameters.description: Optional quote direction. `rm` = MYR per unit foreign currency (default). `fc` = foreign currency per MYR.
parameters.name: quote

schema.description: Optional quote direction. `rm` = MYR per unit foreign currency (default). `fc` = foreign currency per MYR.

endpoints.description: Daily conventional interbank money-market rates by product and tenor. Returns three product buckets: `overall`, `interbank`, and `money_market_operations`. Tenors include overnight, 1-week, 1-month, 3-month, 6-month, and 1-year (nulls indicate no transactions that day). This feed is BNM's successor to the retired KLIBOR benchmark.

endpoints.description: Malaysian Islamic interbank money-market reference rate by tenor (overnight, 1-week, 1-month, 3-month, 6-month, 1-year). endpoints.description:
Preliminary schedule of upcoming bond auctions (group `AUC_SCHED`). Each row carries auction date, delivery date, further-details release date, term, maturity, and auction type (`Bond - Nominal`, `Bond - Real Return`, `T-Bill`, etc.).

Rows are keyed by the `bond_sched` field (date plus term). Schedule is refreshed quarterly alongside the Government of Canada Debt Management Strategy.

schema.description: Number of most recent scheduled auctions to return.

endpoints.description:
Bank of Canada assets and liabilities at month-end (group `B1_MONTHLY`). Covers Government of Canada securities held, foreign-currency claims, Members of Payments Canada deposits, notes in circulation, and capital. Reports the footprint of monetary-policy operations including the evolution through quantitative easing (2020 to 2022) and subsequent balance-sheet normalization.

For weekly detail use the `B2_TABLE` group via `/boc/group/B2_TABLE`.

parameters.name: recent_years

schema.description: Number of years of balance-sheet history.

endpoints.description: The Bank Rate is the upper bound of the operating band for the overnight rate and the rate charged on Standing Liquidity Facility advances to Large Value Transfer System participants. Since the introduction of the operating band it sits 25 basis points above the overnight rate target. This endpoint returns the monthly average overnight rate (`V122514`) and the policy target (`V39079`) for Bank Rate context.

Posted rates from the group `CHARTERED_BANK_INTEREST` covering the prime lending rate (`V80691311`), 1-year, 3-year, and 5-year rates. Bankers' Acceptance rates followed CDOR fixings that ceased in June 2024; current money-market references moved to Term CORRA. This endpoint surfaces the closest remaining Valet proxies via the chartered-bank posted-rate table.

Historical nominal-bond auction results (group `AUC_BOND_RESULTS`). Each row is a single auction with auction date, bidding deadline, issue date, term, maturity, coupon rate, ISIN, amount issued (CAD millions), bid-to-cover ratio, and allotment details.

Rows are keyed by the `bond_id` field (e.g. `2024-01-25_HIST_BOND_1`). Stretches back to 1998.

parameters.description: Number of most recent auction records to return.

endpoints.summary: Government of Canada nominal bond auction results

endpoints.description:
Bank of Canada Business Outlook Survey (group `bos`). Quarterly survey of about 100 senior business executives on past and future sales, investment intentions, hiring, prices, and inflation expectations. Reported as balance-of-opinion indicators (share responding 'up' minus share responding 'down').

Latest results are published one to two weeks after quarter-end, aligning with the Monetary Policy Report cycle.

schema.description: Number of years of survey history.

endpoints.description:
Curated index of Bank of Canada series and groups recommended by Sugra, grouped by theme (rates and monetary policy, yields, FX, inflation, money and credit, commodities, surveys, auctions, stability, balance sheet, climate). Useful as a discovery surface before calling `/boc/series/{seriesCode}` or `/boc/group/{groupName}` directly.

For the full (about 2,300 groups, tens of thousands of series) upstream catalog, call `fetch_series_list()` or `fetch_groups_list()` in the client module.

Climate transition-scenario data (group `CLIMATE_CHANGE`). Global GDP-weighted shadow carbon price, greenhouse-gas emissions path, and sectoral emissions trajectories used in the Bank of Canada's climate-related stress-test scenarios. Aligned with the transition pathways published in the 2022 Climate-Related Risks disclosure.

Additional TCFD balance-sheet disclosures sit in group `TCFD_2024`, accessible via `/boc/group/TCFD_2024`.

endpoints.description:
Monthly Bank of Canada Commodity Price Index (BCPI) and its subcomponents (group `BCPI_MONTHLY`):

- `M.BCPI` - total commodity index
- `M.BCNE` - excluding energy
- `M.ENER` - energy subcomponent (crude oil, natural gas, coal)
- `M.MTLS` - metals and minerals
- `M.AGRI` - agriculture
- `M.FOPR` - forestry
- `M.FISH` - fish

Index, 2010 = 100, fixed-weight basket representative of Canadian commodity production. Used as a leading indicator for Canadian terms of trade.

endpoints.description:
Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations (group `CSCE`). Quarterly survey of about 2,000 household heads on inflation expectations, labour market conditions, household income and spending, and financial vulnerability. Results feed into the Bank of Canada's assessment of expected inflation anchoring.

Published alongside the Monetary Policy Report each quarter.

parameters.description: Number of years of survey history.
parameters.name: recent_years

schema.description: Number of years of survey history.
schema.title: Recent Years

endpoints.description:
CORRA (`AVG.INTWO`) is the daily volume-weighted average of one-day general collateral overnight repo transactions. It replaced CDOR as the recommended Canadian risk-free benchmark and is published each business day around 15:00 ET. Values typically track the overnight rate target closely.

Observations are daily on settlement days and historic coverage begins 1997-08-12.

parameters.description: Number of months of CORRA history.
parameters.name: recent_months

schema.description: Number of months of CORRA history.
schema.title: Recent Months

endpoints.summary: CORRA - Canadian Overnight Repo Rate Average

endpoints.description:
Total CPI year-over-year (`STATIC_ATABLE_V41690973`) plus the three core-inflation measures the Bank of Canada monitors:

- `CPI_TRIM` - CPI-trim, trims 20% of outlier prices each month
- `CPI_MEDIAN` - CPI-median, the 50th-percentile year-over-year change across the basket
- `CPI_COMMON` - CPI-common, factor-analytic estimate of common price movements

All monthly, published by Statistics Canada and mirrored via the Valet API. Bank of Canada targets 2% total CPI with a 1% to 3% band.

parameters.description: Number of years of CPI history.
parameters.name: recent_years

schema.description: Number of years of CPI history.
schema.title: Recent Years

endpoints.summary: Consumer Price Index and core measures

Complements `/boc/mortgage-rates` which returns the rates charged on the same lending categories.

endpoints.description:
Financial Vulnerability Index indicator - loan-to-income ratio (group `FVI_LOAN_TO_INCOME_RATIO`). Reports the share of new mortgage originations with a loan-to-income ratio above 450% and the median LTI across first-time home buyers, repeat buyers, investors, insured and uninsured mortgages.

Published alongside the Financial Stability Report. Companion endpoints on the Sugra group passthrough can access other FVI groups directly: `FVI_LOAN_TO_VALUE_RATIO`, `FVI_BANKS_CA`, `FVI_BANKS_AQ`, `FVI_CREA_RESALE`, `FVI_MORTGAGE_DEBT_SERVICE_RATIO`.

Bank of Canada daily CAD foreign-exchange noon rates for 16 major currencies: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY, CHF, AUD, MXN, INR, BRL, HKD, SGD, SEK, KRW, NOK, NZD. All expressed as CAD per unit of the counter currency (e.g. `FXUSDCAD` = CAD paid for one USD). Published at 16:30 ET on each business day.

endpoints.description:
Forward CAD exchange rates at 3-month, 6-month, and 12-month tenors. Published by the Bank of Canada alongside the spot FX series. Spread to spot reveals interest-rate differentials through covered interest-rate parity.

Forward-rate coverage is narrower than spot and is only reported for the major USD, EUR, GBP and JPY crosses. Returned as a passthrough of the `FX_RATES_DAILY` group for extensibility - spot rates are also included.

Daily noon-rate history for a single CAD FX pair. Accepts any Valet FX code of the form `FX<CCY>CAD` (e.g. `FXUSDCAD`, `FXEURCAD`, `FXJPYCAD`). For the full list of valid pairs, call `/api/v1/boc/fx` or `/api/v1/boc/catalog`.

Returns CAD paid per unit of the counter currency.

Fetch observations for any Bank of Canada Valet group by name. Accepts any group published at `/observations/group/<groupName>/json` - the Valet catalog currently exposes over 2,000 groups including monetary aggregates, FX baskets, bond yields, auctions, surveys, and Financial Vulnerability Index panels.

See `/boc/catalog` for a curated starter list. Time-window parameters mirror the Valet API (only one of `recent`, `recent_weeks`, `recent_months`, `recent_years` or `start_date`/`end_date` should be specified).

endpoints.description:
Canadian monetary aggregates in seasonally adjusted CAD millions:

- `V37151` - M1+ (currency plus personal chequable deposits)
- `V37152` - M1++ (M1+ plus non-personal chequable deposits)
- `V41552801` - M2++ (broadest measure, incl. mutual fund balances)

Year-over-year growth rates are published alongside the levels via `STATIC_ATABLE_V37151`, `STATIC_ATABLE_V37152`, and `STATIC_ATABLE_V41552801` from group `ATABLE_MONETARY_AGGREGATES`. Weekly cadence for M1+ and M1++, monthly for M2++.

Interest rates charged by chartered banks for new and existing mortgage lending (group `A4_RATES_MORTGAGES`). Covers insured and uninsured residential mortgages at variable, short-fixed (<1y), 1-3y, 3-5y, and 5y+ fixed terms.

Month-end observations in percent. Derived from the regulatory data chartered banks file with OSFI and aggregated by the Bank of Canada.

endpoints.description:
Target for the overnight rate (series `V39079`, end-of-month) alongside the monthly average overnight rate (`V122514`). The overnight rate target is the Bank of Canada's primary monetary-policy instrument, set eight times per year at scheduled decisions plus ad-hoc moves when warranted.

Decision dates follow the published Bank of Canada calendar; values change in increments of 25 basis points as the normal rule.

endpoints.description:
Prime rate (`V80691311`) is the base reference banks use to price variable-rate consumer and business loans. The Bank of Canada publishes a daily value based on the rates posted by the six largest chartered banks. Typically moves in 25 bp steps in tandem with the overnight rate target.

Daily business-day resolution.

endpoints.description:
Real Return Bond (RRB) yield (`BD.CDN.RRB.DQ.YLD`) and the long-term nominal benchmark (`BD.CDN.LONG.DQ.YLD`). The spread between the two approximates break-even inflation expectations at the long end of the curve.

RRB issuance was discontinued in November 2022 for new primary-market auctions; secondary-market yields continue to be published.

Senior Loan Officer Survey (group `SLOS`). Quarterly survey of chartered-bank senior loan officers on business and household lending conditions - pricing, non-price terms, and demand. Reported as balance-of-opinion indicators.

Sub-indicators track mortgage lending, non-mortgage household lending, and business lending conditions separately.

Use for money-market conditions, short-rate expectations, and term-structure construction.

Daily benchmark bond yields across the Government of Canada curve: 2-year (`BD.CDN.2YR.DQ.YLD`), 3-year, 5-year, 7-year, 10-year, and long-term (`BD.CDN.LONG.DQ.YLD`, typically 30-year equivalent). Published at 12:15 to 12:30 ET on each business day.

Use for term-premium analysis, curve inversion signals, and real-vs-nominal yield spreads against the Real Return Bond series.

Daily OHLC history for any of 35 CBOE indices. Volatility: VIX, RVX, VVIX, GVZ, OVX. Market: SPX, RUT, OEX, DJX, MXEA, MXEF, SKEW. Strategy: BXM (buy-write), PUT (protection), CLL (collar), CNDR (iron condor), BFLY (iron butterfly), CMBO (combo).
Catalog of all 35 CBOE indices: volatility (9), market (8), strategy (13+).

15-minute delayed options quotes with full Greeks: delta, gamma, vega, theta, rho, implied volatility, theoretical price. Available for any US optionable stock or index. Symbols: AAPL, TSLA, SPY, _VIX (prefix _ for indices).
S&P 500 Volatility Index (VIX) - daily OHLC history since 1990. VIX < 15: low fear. VIX 15-25: normal. VIX 25-35: elevated. VIX > 35: extreme fear. The most watched volatility indicator in the world.
VIX across timeframes: 9-day, 30-day, 3-month, 6-month, 1-year. Contango (long > short) = normal. Backwardation (short > long) = market stress. Key signal for volatility traders and risk managers.
Latest values for all CBOE volatility indices: VIX (S&P 500), RVX (Russell 2000), VVIX (vol of vol), GVZ (gold), OVX (oil), plus VIX term structure variants (9D, 3M, 6M, 1Y).
American Community Survey data for all US states. Includes population, income, housing, poverty, education, employment. Use /census/variables for available variable keys.
List all available demographic and economic variables with codes.

Curated Argentine money-stock series. BCRA does not publish a clean M0/M1/M2/M3 hierarchy the same way most central banks do. This endpoint surfaces the Principales Variables components so clients can assemble their preferred aggregate: base monetaria (15 = M0), circulacion (16), billetes en poder del publico (17), cuentas corrientes privadas (22), cajas de ahorro privadas (23), depositos en efectivo (21), and depositos a plazo (24). All values in ARS millions.

Full daily FX basket snapshot published by BCRA Estadisticas Cambiarias - every supported currency (44 on a typical business day) priced against ARS for a single fecha. Each row carries tipo_pase (cross to USD) and tipo_cotizacion (ARS per unit). Without a date parameter upstream returns the latest published snapshot (usually T-1 after the wholesale close ~15:00 ART).

Master list of currencies supported by BCRA Estadisticas Cambiarias - 44 entries covering live FIAT (USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CHF, CAD, AUD, CNY, BRL, CLP, UYU, PYG, BOB, COP, PEN, MXP, HKD, SGD, NZD, DKK, NOK, SEK, CZK, INR, RSD, RUB, TRY, ZAR, ILS, VND and more), legacy FIAT (ATS, DEM, ESP, ITL, VEB), precious metals (XAU gold, XAG silver), and SDR (XDR / DEG). Deduplicates the duplicate NIO row published upstream.

Daily ARS quote for a single currency published by BCRA Estadisticas Cambiarias. Without date bounds upstream returns only the latest published snapshot. Supports start_date and end_date query params for a bounded history window. Each row carries tipo_pase (cross to USD) and tipo_cotizacion (ARS per unit of the quoted currency).

endpoints.description: Argentine IPC (Indice de Precios al Consumidor) together with monthly market expectations from the REM survey. Returns three series: IPC month-on-month (idVariable 27), IPC year-on-year (idVariable 28), and the median REM 12-month-ahead inflation expectation (idVariable 29). All monthly, published mid-month by INDEC and aggregated by BCRA.

Full catalog of 1220 BCRA Principal Variables and related monetary families. Each entry carries idVariable, descripcion (Spanish), categoria, periodicidad, unidad_expresion, first and last reporting dates, and the latest reported value. Use this to discover series before calling /monetary/{series_id}/history.

Metadata for a single Monetarias variable (descripcion, categoria, periodicidad, unidad_expresion, first and last reporting date, latest value). Returns 404 when the idVariable is not in the published catalog.

Observation time series for any Monetarias variable (1220 supported). Supports date-bounded queries via start_date and end_date. Values are returned with the original BCRA precision (floats). Non-business days are absent from the sequence - upstream publishes no null fills.

Argentina has no single 'policy rate' series; since late 2024 the de-facto reference is the TAMAR on private-bank 30-day fixed-term deposits (idVariable 44). This convenience card returns the six key rates - TAMAR, BADLAR (7), BAIBAR (11), plazo fijo 30d (12), adelantos (13), prestamos personales (14) - in a single payload.
Daily international reserves held by Banco Central de la Republica Argentina, in USD millions. Source: BCRA Monetarias idVariable 1. History since 1996-01-03.

Values reflect the latest observation per series.

The 27 SARB regulatory Institutional Form types (BA100 Balance Sheet, BA120 Income Statement, BA325 Market Risk, BA330 Interest-Rate Risk, BA900 Capital Adequacy, etc.) with last-update metadata and SARB contact information. Each form is a prudential reporting template filed by licensed banks. Source: South African Reserve Bank (SARB).

SARB series discovery index aggregated from /ReleaseOfSelectedData (17 upstream data types with last-release timestamps) plus the Sugra curated list of convenience codes used by named endpoints. Source: South African Reserve Bank (SARB).

Monthly time series of M3 broad-money aggregate year-over-year change published by the South African Reserve Bank (SARB) as upstream code `MON0300P`. M3 is the broadest SARB monetary aggregate, including notes, coin, sight deposits, medium-term and long-term deposits.

Daily time series of the South African Reserve Bank (SARB) policy rate (repo rate) - the interest rate set by the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) to achieve the inflation target. Upstream code `MMRD002A`. Observations sorted oldest-first.

Daily time series of the South African prime lending rate - the rate commercial banks charge their most creditworthy customers. Published by SARB as upstream code `MMRD000A`. Typically 3.5 percentage points above the policy rate.

Audit trail of prime and repo (policy) rate changes published by the South African Reserve Bank (SARB). Each row records the date of an MPC decision and the new rate value. Covers two upstream series: `MRDPRIME` (prime) and `MRDREPOR` (repo).
endpoints.summary: Historical MPC rate changes (repo and prime)

endpoints.description: Daily time series of Sabor (South African Benchmark Overnight Rate) - the overnight interbank reference rate computed by the South African Reserve Bank (SARB). Sabor is being phased out in favour of Zaronia. Upstream code `MMRD851A`.

Preview rows (first 20) from each of the seven SARB SDDS (IMF Special Data Dissemination Standard) sectoral cubes: Real Sector (GDP composition), External Sector (BOP), Financial Sector (Depository Corporations Survey), Fiscal Sector (General Government Operations), Business Cycles, Population, and Footnotes. Use `/series/{quote_name}` with the TimeseriesCode from any sample row to fetch deep history. Source: South African Reserve Bank (SARB).

Fetch any South African Reserve Bank (SARB) timeseries by its TimeseriesCode. Accepts any code published by SARB (e.g. `MMRD002A` policy rate, `CPI1000F` CPI, `EXCX135D` ZAR/USD). SARB returns rows in reverse-chronological order; Sugra sorts ascending before returning. Invalid codes return an empty `observations` list.

endpoints.description: Latest Czech National Bank policy rates extracted from the CNB open market operations (OMO) feed. Returns all three most recent rows together with the effective trade date.

endpoints.description: CNB open market operations history across a date range. Returns repo (2W policy rate), deposit facility and Lombard auction rows with full bid / allotment statistics. Sourced from `/cnbapi/omo/daily-year`, looped across the requested years and filtered by trade date.

Sums S122 banks + S123 MMF + S124 investment funds + S128 insurance + S129 pension debt holdings per period for one country. Cross-store join across W6 SHSS S124 and D SHSS multi-sector helpers - composes their public APIs without a separate ingest path or schema bump.

MIN-anchored upper period bound + best-effort fill below ensures per-sector publication skew does not produce a sawtooth right-edge or strict-all-5 history truncation. Each row's `n_sectors_with_data` field surfaces how many sectors contributed to that period's total.

`include_breakdown=false` drops per-sector values from each row (smaller payload, less transparency). Cached 5 minutes via cache_async_lock_for double-check (mirror of /cross/transatlantic).

Get the full directory of supported coins. Each entry returns `id` (slug), `symbol`,
and `name`. Use the `id` value as `coin_id` when calling other `/crypto` endpoints.

This is a large response (10,000+ rows) - cache it locally and refresh daily rather than
calling it on every lookup.
endpoints.summary: List all supported coins (id, symbol, name)

endpoints.description: Active advertisement placements on the on-chain DEX market. Each record reports the advertised token (chain + address), the on-chain DEX page URL the ad targets, the ad type (e.g. tokenAd), impression count reported by upstream, and the ad publish date (ISO 8601 UTC). Useful for monitoring promoted tokens appearing on the on-chain DEX front page. Sourced from on-chain DEX data.

Recent community-takeover events on the on-chain DEX market. A community takeover happens when a non-team group claims stewardship of an abandoned or rugged token and publishes a fresh profile page with new social links. Each record reports the token (chain + address), the new profile imagery and description, community-supplied links, and the claim timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC). Sourced from on-chain DEX data.

Trending on-chain market segments curated on-chain. Segments cluster tokens by narrative (Meme Hall of Fame, AI, DeFi, L2s, regional meme sub-categories) and each record reports aggregate market cap, liquidity, 24-hour volume, token count, and rolling market-cap percent-change / delta over m5 / h1 / h6 / h24 windows. Use the `slug` on each summary to drill into the full pair list via `/market-segments/{slug}`.

Detail view of a single on-chain DEX market segment by slug. Returns the segment's aggregate market cap, liquidity, 24-hour volume, token count, rolling market-cap percent-change / delta, plus the full pair list for every token in the segment with live on-chain prices, transaction counts, and liquidity. Use `/market-segments/trending` to discover available slugs. Sourced from on-chain DEX data.
endpoints.summary: Market segment detail with full pair list

endpoints.description: Paid orders and promotional history for a specific token on the on-chain DEX market - token profile submissions, advertisement placements, boost purchases, and community-takeover claims. Each record reports the order type, upstream status (processing, approved, cancelled, rejected), and payment timestamp (UNIX milliseconds). Useful as a compliance / disclosure surface before interacting with a promoted token.

endpoints.description: Daily Crypto Fear & Greed Index observations from Alternative.me, the originating publisher of the composite sentiment indicator. The 0-100 score weights volatility (25%), market momentum and volume (25%), social media (15%), Bitcoin dominance (10%), trends (10%), and surveys when available (15%). The index refreshes once per day near 00:00 UTC; Sugra caches responses for 24 hours. Licence: CC BY 4.0.

endpoints.description: Fetch full dataset metadata and embedded resource URLs via CKAN `package_show`. Resource URLs point to the publishing department's host (HKO, EPD, Transport Department, Hospital Authority etc.); Sugra passes them through unchanged - download resources directly from upstream.

List datasets on the Hong Kong portal. When `group` is supplied, the response includes full package summaries for datasets in that group (CKAN `group_show?include_datasets=true`). With no `group` filter, the response returns the complete bare-slug index via CKAN `package_list` (no metadata). Upstream does not expose `package_search`; use `group` or a dataset ID on `/dataset/{id}` to retrieve metadata.

parameters.description: Optional group slug filter (see `/data-gov-hk/groups`). When omitted, returns the full slug index.
parameters.name: group

schema.description: Optional group slug filter (see `/data-gov-hk/groups`). When omitted, returns the full slug index.
schema.title: Group

endpoints.description: List the 20 top-level category groups on data.gov.hk (e.g. `finance`, `climate-and-weather`, `transport`, `environment`). Groups are the navigation taxonomy used across the portal. The `lang` parameter selects the English, Traditional Chinese, or Simplified Chinese parallel CKAN host; the group slugs themselves are language-neutral.

Accident & Emergency waiting times across public hospitals operated by the Hong Kong Hospital Authority. Returns per-hospital wait snapshots for triage Categories I (critical) and II (emergency), plus median (P50) and 95th-percentile (P95) waits for Categories III, IV, and V. Upstream refreshes every 15 minutes; Sugra caches for 10 minutes.

Current weather readings from the Hong Kong Observatory Regional Hourly Reading API (dataType=rhrread). Includes temperatures at 27 automatic weather stations, relative humidity at the Hong Kong Observatory headquarters, rainfall totals by district, the UV index bucket, and the icon code summarising conditions. Upstream refreshes every 10 minutes; Sugra caches for 5 minutes.

Nine-day daily forecast from the Hong Kong Observatory (dataType=fnd). Each day includes maximum / minimum temperature, maximum / minimum relative humidity, wind description, weather narrative, icon code, and the probability-of-significant-rain bucket. The response also carries the general-situation narrative and spot sea / soil temperature readings. Upstream refreshes hourly; Sugra caches for 10 minutes.

Active Hong Kong Observatory warnings (dataType=warnsum). Covers tropical cyclone signals (T1 / T3 / T8 / T9 / T10), rainstorm warnings (Amber / Red / Black), the thunderstorm warning, strong monsoon signal, flood warnings, landslip warnings, and other public-safety advisories. Returns `active_count = 0` with an empty `warnings` list when no warning is in force. Upstream is event-driven; Sugra caches for 3 minutes.

Next-train schedule for Hong Kong MTR heavy-rail lines. Returns up to four upcoming arrivals in each direction (UP / DOWN) at the requested station. The `line` parameter uses MTR line codes (`TKL` = Tseung Kwan O, `TWL` = Tsuen Wan, `ISL` = Island, `KTL` = Kwun Tong, `EAL` = East Rail, `TML` = Tuen Ma, `SIL` = South Island, `DRL` = Disneyland Resort, `AEL` = Airport Express). Upstream returns `sys_time` / `curr_time` in naive HKT; Sugra converts both to UTC. Upstream refresh cadence is 10-30 s; Sugra caches for 5 minutes to match the shortest cache bucket.

Segment-level traffic speed across the Hong Kong strategic road network, published by the Transport Department. Response contains per-segment speed observations, a validity flag (`Y` = observed, `N` = default / posted limit) and the TD IRN version. Sugra computes the mean speed across segments flagged valid. Upstream refreshes every two minutes; Sugra caches for five minutes.

DEX aggregator trading volumes - 1inch, Jupiter, Paraswap, CowSwap, etc. 130+ aggregators. Shows routing volume across DEX protocols. Aggregator volume is distinct from DEX volume (same trade counted differently).

endpoints.description: List all available ECB Data Portal dataset keys with descriptions.

Full historical insurance balance-sheet timeseries for one country, split between stock observations (closing-balance positions) and flow observations (quarterly transactions). All balance-sheet items (T00 + assets + liabilities) returned in a single timeseries; consumers filter by `item` field. Slug is 2-char lowercase: `at`/`de`/`fr`/`lu`/`ie` etc. for euro-area members; `u2` for the euro-area aggregate. Both timeseries are sorted by period descending (latest first).
endpoints.summary: Single country full insurance balance-sheet timeseries (all items)

endpoints.description: Euro-area countries ranked by absolute change in insurance balance sheet size (T00) across periods. `period=qoq` compares latest quarter to one quarter back; `period=yoy` to four quarters back. Sort is by absolute delta descending so both fastest accumulators and biggest contractors surface together. Excludes U2 (would self-compare) and reporters with gaps in the comparison window.
endpoints.summary: Top movers by insurance balance sheet delta (qoq/yoy)

endpoints.description: Asset-class composition of the euro-area insurance balance sheet at U2 aggregate latest_period. Returns each numerator item (A10 currency, A20 loans, A30 debt securities held, A40 insurance technical reserves asset side, A50 equity, A60 investment fund shares, A70 derivatives, A80 non-financial assets, A90 remaining assets) with its share of T00 (total assets, the unconditional denominator). Rows sorted by share descending; sum approaches 100% within ESA 2010 balance-sheet identity tolerance.
endpoints.summary: Euro-area insurance balance sheet asset composition (U2 latest)

endpoints.description: Latest-quarter balance-sheet snapshot for the euro-area insurance sector (S128) at the U2 aggregate level. Returns total assets/liabilities (T00) plus per-item breakdown across asset codes (A10/A20/A30/A40/A50/A60/A70/A80/A90) and liability codes (L20/L30/L40/L50/L70/L90). Asset items sum to T00 and liability items sum to T00 within ESA 2010 balance-sheet identity tolerance (~2% upper bound; observed residuals well below 1%). `total_source` indicates U2 aggregate vs member-state-sum fallback. Source: ECB Insurance Corporations Assets and Liabilities dataflow (ICB).
endpoints.summary: Latest euro-area insurance balance sheet (U2 aggregate)

endpoints.description: Top N euro-area countries ranked by total insurance balance sheet (T00, total assets / total liabilities; equal by accounting identity) at the latest reported quarter. Excludes the U2 euro-area aggregate (use `/icb/insurance/by-country/u2` for the aggregate timeseries). Default `n=10`.
endpoints.summary: Top N euro-area countries by insurance balance sheet size

endpoints.description: Full historical IVF timeseries for one country across all 4 sectors x 6 items, split between stock observations (data_type=1, closing balances) and flow observations (data_type=4, monthly transactions). Slug is 2-char lowercase: at, de, fr, lu, ie etc. for euro-area members; u2 for the euro-area aggregate (mirror SHS - U2 is accessible via this endpoint, only excluded from /top and /changes and /flows leaderboards). ?include=composition adds asset-mix breakdown for sector=T0 (all funds) at latest period: debt_pct + direct_equity_pct + fund_shares_pct + cash_pct of L30 NAV + leverage_ratio (T00/L30). When NAV is null or non-positive, percentages = null.

endpoints.description: Full IVF historical timeseries for one sector cross-cut. Default returns the U2 euro-area aggregate only (cleaner default response). ?include_country_breakdown=true adds per-country rows for the sector. Sectors: ucits (TC = all UCITS funds cross-cut, NOT 1D-equity-only as in some legacy ECB documentation), aif (TD = non-UCITS = AIF cross-cut), hedge (50 = hedge funds), total (T0 = all investment funds). Hedge sector data is sparse; per-country values may be null - opt into ?include_country_breakdown=true explicitly to surface per-country sparseness.

endpoints.description: Countries ranked by absolute change in selected metric across periods. ?period=mom compares the latest month to one month back; ?period=yoy compares to twelve months back. Sort is by absolute delta descending so both biggest accumulators and biggest divestors appear at the top. Excludes the U2 aggregate (would self-compare) and skips countries with insufficient history. Filters defunct entries by latest_period match (W5 staging-smoke pattern).
endpoints.summary: Top movers leaderboard - NAV or total-assets delta

endpoints.description: Countries ranked by absolute NAV transaction-flow magnitude. ?period=latest returns each country's most recent monthly flow value; ?period=yoy aggregates the last twelve months per country. Positive flow = net subscriptions (new money in); negative = net redemptions (money out). Distinct from /changes (stock delta) because flow data excludes valuation effects - it is pure investor-behavior signal. NAV-based only in v1 (multi-instrument flows deferred to v2). Excludes the U2 aggregate.

endpoints.description: Discovery endpoint for the Our World in Data CO2 and Greenhouse Gas Emissions bundle. Lists every entity in the dataset (254 on the December 2025 vintage), the year range covered, every metric column name, and the whitelist of region aggregates accepted by `/region/{region}`. Use this before calling `/country/{iso3}`, `/country/{iso3}/{metric}`, or `/top` to confirm the exact metric spelling and the ISO codes for the countries of interest. Published under CC BY 4.0 - dual attribution to Our World in Data and the primary upstream providers named in the codebook (Global Carbon Project, Jones et al., EIA, Energy Institute, Maddison Project, OWID Population).
endpoints.summary: Catalog of entities, year range, metrics, and regions

endpoints.description: Returns the column dictionary shipped with the bundle - one row per column with title, description, unit, and the primary upstream source. The codebook is OWID's authoritative schema definition and is the correct place to discover available metrics, their units, and methodological caveats before using them in `/country/{iso3}/{metric}` or `/top`. Published under CC BY 4.0 with dual attribution to Our World in Data and the primary upstream providers named per column.
endpoints.summary: Full column dictionary for the OWID CO2 bundle

endpoints.description: Returns every column in the OWID CO2 bundle for one ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 coded country. Covers CO2 totals, CO2 by source (coal, oil, gas, cement, flaring, other, land-use change), consumption-based CO2, cumulative CO2, share-of-global contributions, methane, nitrous oxide, total greenhouse gases, temperature-change contributions, population, GDP, and primary energy. Annual cadence. Early rows (pre-1900) carry only cement and basic CO2 columns; later decades populate progressively more columns as upstream measurement starts. For regional aggregates (World, EU27, OECD, Africa) use `/world` or `/region/{region}` - ISO3 coverage is for sovereign countries only. Published under CC BY 4.0 with dual attribution to Our World in Data and the per-column primary provider.

endpoints.description: Index of per-layer ColorMap_v1.3 XML documents published at `https://gibs.earthdata.nasa.gov/colormaps/v1.3/`. Each entry maps a layer identifier to the colormap URL; clients dereference the URL to obtain RGB stops, value ranges, and legend metadata. Cached 7 days - colormaps change rarely once a layer ships. Data: NASA GIBS / NASA Earthdata (US public domain).
endpoints.summary: List NASA GIBS colormap XML documents

endpoints.description: Full metadata record for a single GIBS layer. Includes title, MIME format, tile matrix set, WGS84 bounding box, every declared ISO 8601 time interval, the RESTful tile URL template (placeholders intact), metadata links (colormap versions, CMR collection DOIs), and horizontal plus vertical SVG legend URLs. Also reports which other GIBS projections this layer appears in. Data: NASA GIBS / NASA Earthdata (US public domain).
endpoints.summary: Detailed metadata for one NASA GIBS layer

endpoints.description: Build a tile URL for the NASA GIBS CDN by substituting `time`, `z`, `x`, `y` (and optionally `format` / `projection`) into the layer's ResourceURL template. Returns a JSON record with the constructed URL; clients must fetch the tile directly from the NASA GIBS CloudFront CDN. Sugra does NOT proxy binary tiles: tile fetches through Sugra would cost bandwidth on the Azure App Service for zero added value given NASA's global edge network. Data: NASA GIBS / NASA Earthdata (US public domain).

parameters.description: TileMatrix (zoom level), 0-12 (NASA GIBS caps 250 m imagery at z=9).

endpoints.description: Available timestamps for the layer. Preferentially fetches the per-layer Domains XML (authoritative, cached 6 hours) which NASA GIBS generates from the backing tile index. Falls back to the intervals declared in the WMTS capabilities document if Domains is unavailable. Intervals follow ISO 8601 (`start/end/period` with `P1D` daily, `P1M` monthly, `P8D` 8-day, etc). Multiple intervals indicate instrument gaps. Data: NASA GIBS / NASA Earthdata (US public domain).

endpoints.description: Paginated catalog of NASA GIBS layers parsed from the upstream WMTS capabilities document (4.93 MB XML, cached 24 hours). 1236 layers are published on the default `epsg4326` projection, spanning MODIS, VIIRS, AIRS, OMI, OMPS, CERES, AMSR, SMAP, MERRA-2, TEMPO, MISR, Landsat, and more. Filter by `q` (substring against identifier and title), `format` (`image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `application/vnd.mapbox-vector-tile`), and `projection`. Sugra does NOT proxy binary tiles. Data: NASA GIBS / NASA Earthdata (US public domain).

endpoints.description: Static catalog of the four projections NASA GIBS serves via WMTS: `epsg4326` (Geographic / WGS84, global lat-lon), `epsg3857` (Web Mercator), `epsg3413` (NSIDC Sea Ice Polar Stereographic North, Arctic), `epsg3031` (Antarctic Polar Stereographic). Each entry also reports the WMTSCapabilities URL and the set of TileMatrixSets (resolution tiers) declared in the projection when the catalog has already been cached. Data: NASA GIBS / NASA Earthdata (US public domain).

Single-shot convenience endpoint: the latest observation at a given station for a given product. Default product is `water_level`. Equivalent to calling `/station/{id}/water-level?date=latest` with fewer ceremonial arguments. Returns `observation=null` when the station does not report the requested product. Attribution: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents (api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov).

endpoints.description: Return fused metadata for a single CO-OPS station: identity, tidal datums (MLLW, MSL, MHW, MHHW, NAVD88, and others when published) with reference epoch, instrumentation inventory (sensor IDs, elevation, reference datum, status), and a summary of the harmonic analysis (units and constituent count). One Sugra call fuses four upstream mdapi sub-resources. Attribution: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents (api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov).

parameters.description: 7-digit CO-OPS station identifier (e.g. `8518750` for The Battery, `9414290` for San Francisco).
parameters.name: id

schema.description: 7-digit CO-OPS station identifier (e.g. `8518750` for The Battery, `9414290` for San Francisco).
schema.title: Id

endpoints.description: Published harmonic constituents for a CO-OPS station - 37 classical tidal constituents including M2, S2, N2, K1, O1, M4, K2, and others - with amplitude, phase (GMT and local), and angular speed. These are the inputs the prediction engine uses to synthesize tide levels, and are published so downstream researchers can reproduce predictions independently. Attribution: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents (api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov).

High/low tide events at a CO-OPS station, derived from the harmonic tide prediction engine. Each event carries the timestamp, predicted level, and a type marker `H` (high) or `L` (low). Convenience wrapper over `/predictions?interval=hilo`. Attribution: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents (api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov).

Meteorological sensor observations from the CO-OPS gauge. Select one product via the `product` query parameter: `wind`, `air_pressure`, `air_temperature`, `humidity`, or `visibility`. Not every sensor is installed at every station; the station-detail endpoint lists the sensor inventory. When the requested product is not instrumented at the station, Sugra returns 404 with the upstream explanation. Attribution: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents (api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov).
endpoints.summary: Meteorological observations co-located with tide gauge

endpoints.description: Harmonic-analysis tide predictions at a CO-OPS station. Predictions are deterministic from the station's harmonic constants and run 12+ months ahead and into the past. Default datum MLLW, units metric. Interval values: `6` (six-minute, default), `h` (hourly), `hilo` (high/low events only). For high/low events prefer the dedicated `/high-low` endpoint. Attribution: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents (api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov).

Return tide-gauge water-level observations at a CO-OPS station. Default datum is MLLW (Mean Lower-Low Water, the US navigational default) and units metric (meters). Requests without `begin_date`/`end_date` default to today's 6-minute observations. Requests with an explicit range are capped at 31 days for 6-minute data and 365 days for hourly (`interval=h`). `q` quality flag: `p` preliminary (last 30-45 days), `v` verified (older, passed CO-OPS QC). Attribution: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents (api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov).

endpoints.description: Water temperature observations at a CO-OPS station. Distinct endpoint rather than a subset of `/meteorology` because water temperature is an oceanographic variable used in marine biology, fisheries, and coastal thermal modeling - conceptually paired with water level. Default units metric (Celsius). Attribution: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents (api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov).

List CO-OPS stations. Default `type=waterlevels` returns the 301 primary National Water Level Observation Network (NWLON) stations covering all US coasts, the Great Lakes, Alaska, Hawaii, and territories (some with continuous records since 1856). Pass `type=tidepredictions` for the full 3,449-entry prediction catalog (primary stations plus subordinate offsets), `type=currents` for 88 current-meter stations in PORTS projects, or `type=all` for the unfiltered upstream set. Optional `state` 2-letter filter is applied client-side against Sugra's 24-hour in-process catalog cache. Attribution: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents (api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov).

Source: ICI public press release (Investment Company Institute, Tier I non-profit trade association). 6 buckets (Equity Domestic/World, Hybrid, Bond Taxable/Municipal, Commodity) + Total + a separate ETF net issuance figure. endpoints.description: Cross-sector ranking of US sector SPDR ETFs by trailing return over a configurable window. Walks the 11 canonical S&P GICS sector SPDRs (XLB, XLC, XLY, XLP, XLE, XLF, XLV, XLI, XLRE, XLK, XLU), extracts the matching ``returns_<window>`` field from each ETF's latest B5 snapshot (or a dated snapshot via ``?date=YYYY-MM-DD``), and ranks DESC. Sectors missing a return (delisted, snapshot blob absent, or ETF history shorter than the window) are surfaced in ``missing_symbols`` with a structured reason and do not contribute to leader / laggard / spread / median. Use case: ``UC-3 Sector Rotation Detector`` - capital rotation signal without N round-trips.

endpoints.description: List of US ETFs covered by the Sugra Finance fund-summary daily snapshot. Each entry carries the ticker, name, category, AUM rank, and a status flag (``active`` / ``delisted_candidate`` / ``removed``). The universe refreshes quarterly; the daily snapshot cycle populates ``last_snapshot_date`` and ``snapshot_count`` per symbol from the latest manifest.

Derived endpoint that computes per-day net flow estimates for one ETF over the requested rolling window by walking consecutive ETF snapshots. Formula: ``net_flow_today = Δ(shares_outstanding) × nav_today`` where ``shares_outstanding = aum_usd / nav``. This is the standard ETF creation/redemption proxy used by analysts; results are an ESTIMATE, not ICI-authoritative numbers. Days where the snapshot blob is missing are silently skipped (sparse series). The first available day always has ``net_flow_dollar_estimate=null`` (no prior day to diff against). Use case: ``UC-3 Sector Rotation Detector`` per-ETF accumulation signal, complemented by ICI aggregate at /etf/flows/aggregate.

parameters.description: Lookback window in days (1..90). Out-of-range returns 400 per ETF snapshot retention contract.

endpoints.description: Time-series of per-date snapshot projections between ``from_date`` and ``to_date`` (both inclusive). Sparse: dates without a snapshot are represented by a ``null`` entry in ``snapshots``. The ``fields`` CSV parameter restricts the projection to a whitelist; default fields are ``nav,price,premium_discount_pct,aum_usd``. The window may not extend past the 90-day retention boundary nor into the future.

endpoints.description: Diff between the top-10 holdings of two ETF snapshots. Returns three buckets: ``new_top_10`` (appeared in to_date, absent from from_date), ``dropped_from_top_10`` (in from_date, absent from to_date), and ``weight_changed`` (present in both). The mandatory ``caveat`` field discloses that the upstream feed reports a top-10 sample only - drop-outs may remain in the portfolio but fell below the top-10 rank threshold.

Catalog of all 23 Fama-French datasets: factors (3/5/momentum/reversal, monthly and daily), industry portfolios (5 to 49 industries), size/value portfolios (6/25/100), momentum portfolios, characteristic-sorted portfolios.

Academic finance risk factors for asset pricing and portfolio analysis. 3-Factor (1926+): Mkt-RF (market premium), SMB (small minus big), HML (value). 5-Factor (1963+): adds RMW (profitability), CMA (investment). Momentum (1927+): MOM (winners minus losers). Also: short-term reversal, long-term reversal. Monthly and daily frequencies available.

endpoints.summary: All currently disclosed UK short positions (>=0.5%)

endpoints.description: Time-ordered (ascending) merge of current + historical disclosed shorts for one ISIN. Historical archive goes back to 2012-11. Optional filters: `since`/`until` (ISO date) and `holder` (slug) to narrow to a specific fund's history with the stock.

endpoints.description: Every UK stock currently shorted (>=0.5%) by a specific position holder. Slug format: lowercased, alphanumeric + hyphen (e.g. `citadel-europe-llp`, `marshall-wace-llp`, `aqr-capital-management-llc`). Strict slug match - fuzzy holder-name matching may come in a follow-up wave.

endpoints.description: Top N UK-listed stocks ranked by total disclosed short % (sum across all individual holders >=0.5%). Useful for crowded-short / squeeze candidates. Note: only positions >=0.5% are public; positions 0.1%-0.5% are private to FCA.

endpoints.description: Latest-quarter balance-sheet snapshot for the Federal Reserve Z.1 hedge funds sector (622, table B.101.f). 12 instruments side-by-side with current value, reporting period, and FRED series ID. Includes total net assets, total financial and nonfinancial assets, total liabilities, plus asset-side detail (Treasuries, corporate equities, leveraged loans, cash equivalents, long derivatives) and liability-side detail (repo, prime broker borrowing, margin loans). Source: 12 FRED BOGZ1 series fetched in parallel. Cache 3 days. Coverage caveat: domestic US hedge funds only - foreign-domiciled (Cayman, Bahamas) NOT in this sector. Hedge fund tracking started 2014-Q1.
endpoints.summary: Hedge fund balance sheet snapshot (12 instruments)

endpoints.description: Gross leverage ratio over time, derived from Federal Reserve Z.1 hedge funds sector. Formula: total financial and nonfinancial assets / total net assets. Net assets is the equity-component analog (assets minus liabilities). A ratio of 3.0 means the sector holds $3 in gross assets per $1 of equity. Two FRED series fetched in parallel and joined by date. Default last 40 quarters.
endpoints.summary: Hedge fund gross leverage time series

endpoints.description: Time series (last N quarters, ascending) for one instrument from the hedge funds balance sheet. Allowed instrument slugs: total_net_assets, total_assets_finnonfin, total_financial_assets, treasuries, corporate_equities, leveraged_loans, cash_equivalents, derivatives_long, total_liabilities, repo_liability, prime_broker_borrowing, margin_loans. Default last 40 quarters (10 years). All values in Millions of US Dollars.

Static reference: the 13 Federal Reserve Z.1 sectors most relevant to Hedge Fund Intelligence analysis (households, government, corporate, depository institutions, mutual funds, MMF, pension funds, hedge funds, etc.). Use the sector code to navigate FRED for sector-specific BOGZ1 series.
endpoints.summary: Federal Reserve Z.1 sector codes catalog

Generic Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) series proxy. Pass any FRED series_id and receive observations plus metadata. Examples: CPIAUCSL (US CPI inflation), GDP (US GDP), UNRATE (US unemployment rate), DFF (federal funds effective rate), DGS10 (10-year Treasury yield), M2SL (M2 money supply). Covers the full FRED catalog (~800k macroeconomic time series from the St. Louis Fed).

endpoints.description: Return administrative and topological detail for a single OpenStreetMap place. Responses can be very large for famous places when the multilingual `names` map is populated. Intended for one-shot lookups, not live autocomplete. Data is (C) OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL 1.0).

Each value carries a code, human-readable title, and optional parent-dimension pointers (e.g. WHO region for countries). Use this endpoint as a decoder when rows returned by `/data/{code}` carry cryptic `Dim1` / `Dim2` / `Dim3` values.

Use the returned codes with `/dimension/{code}/values` to discover the allowed values for a specific axis.

endpoints.description: Browse the full catalog of 3057 health indicators published by the World Health Organization Global Health Observatory (WHO GHO). Each entry carries a stable indicator code (e.g. `WHOSIS_000001` for life expectancy at birth, `MDG_0000000007` for under-5 mortality) and a human-readable English name. Filter by theme (Sugra-derived from the indicator-code prefix heuristic: `hiv`, `tb`, `ncd`, `tobacco`, `immunization`, `sdg`, and more) or by free-text substring via `q`. Legacy `See ...` alias rows are flagged with `is_alias=true` so clients can filter them out when needed.

endpoints.description: Rank countries by a World Health Organization Global Health Observatory (WHO GHO) indicator for a chosen calendar year. Only rows with `SpatialDimType=COUNTRY` and `Dim1=SEX_BTSX` (both sexes) are considered so the leaderboard is comparable across countries. Use `order=desc` (default) for largest values first (e.g. countries with the highest life expectancy) or `order=asc` for lowest values first (e.g. lowest under-5 mortality).

parameters.description: Indicator code (e.g. `WHOSIS_000001` for life expectancy, `MDG_0000000007` for under-5 mortality).

parameters.description: Sort direction - `desc` for highest values first, `asc` for lowest.
parameters.name: order

schema.description: Sort direction - `desc` for highest values first, `asc` for lowest.
schema.title: Order

endpoints.summary: Top-N country ranking for a WHO GHO indicator and year

endpoints.description: Full-text search across the World Health Organization Global Health Observatory (WHO GHO) indicator catalog. The query string is forwarded to OData `contains(IndicatorName, q)` on upstream and matched case-sensitively. Each hit is returned with its stable indicator code, English-language name, and a Sugra-derived `theme_hint` based on the code-prefix taxonomy.

parameters.description: Search substring matched against `IndicatorName` via OData `contains()`.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search substring matched against `IndicatorName` via OData `contains()`.
schema.title: Q

endpoints.summary: Search WHO GHO indicators by name

endpoints.description: Return the Sugra-derived theme taxonomy used to group the World Health Organization Global Health Observatory (WHO GHO) indicator catalog. WHO does not expose a /Theme endpoint upstream; Sugra synthesises themes from indicator-code prefixes (`HIV_`, `TB_`, `NCD_`, `TOBACCO_`, `MDG_`, `WHOSIS_`, `SDG`, and more). Pass any returned `theme` slug to `/indicators?theme=...` to filter the catalog.
endpoints.summary: Theme taxonomy for the WHO GHO indicator catalog

endpoints.description: Hrvatska kuna (HRK) base reference rates, frozen at 2022-12-31 when Croatia adopted the euro. Each daily list carries 15 entries (AUD, CAD, CZK, DKK, HUF, JPY, NOK, SEK, CHF, GBP, USD, XDR, BAM, EUR, PLN) quoted against HRK, with the `jedinica` field giving the quoting unit (HUF and JPY were historically quoted per 100). Dates from 2023-01-01 onward return an empty list. Source: api.hnb.hr/tecajn/v2.

Sugra applies a workaround by fetching the full panel for the range and filtering by currency client-side. Source: api.hnb.hr/tecajn-eur/v3.

endpoints.description: Croatian National Bank daily reference list - 13 non-EUR currencies quoted per 1 EUR. Published each TARGET2 business day for statutory, accounting and tax-reporting use inside Croatia. The middle rate (srednji_tecaj) is the canonical value and is legally distinct from the ECB euro reference rate. Source: api.hnb.hr/tecajn-eur/v3.

Full 13-currency EUR-base snapshot for a single historical date. Empty list when the requested date is not a TARGET2 business day or predates Eurozone entry (2023-01-02). Response is effectively immutable once produced. Source: api.hnb.hr/tecajn-eur/v3.

endpoints.description: Share of children in child labour, from ILO dataflow DF_CLD_XCHL_SEX_AGE_RT. AGE dimension covers child age bands (e.g. AGE_CLDVERSION_Y05-17 for the full 5-17 definition, Y05-11 and Y12-14 for narrower bands). Source surveys: MICS, DHS, and national labour-force surveys.

Fetch any of the 1,166 ILOSTAT dataflows by identifier. Sugra encodes the dimension order for 20 featured dataflows; for the rest the request is sent with an `all` key and paginated by `last_n`. Use the catalog endpoint to discover valid dataflow identifiers.

Discover ILOSTAT dataflows. Returns Sugra's curated subset of 20 headline indicators by default (unemployment, employment, earnings, hours worked, injuries, labour productivity, child labour, strikes, etc). Pass `full=true` to retrieve the complete 1,166-dataflow catalog from ILO's SDMX registry.

endpoints.description: Mean monthly earnings from ILO dataflow DF_EAR_EMTA_SEX_AGE_CUR_NB. The CUR dimension selects currency conversion: CUR_TYPE_LCU (local currency), CUR_TYPE_USD (US dollar), CUR_TYPE_PPP (PPP-adjusted US dollar), CUR_TYPE_EUR (Euro). Leave `currency` empty to return all currencies side-by-side.

endpoints.description: Share of the working-age population that is employed, from ILO dataflow DF_EMP_DWAP_SEX_AGE_RT. Covers 180+ countries, annual primary frequency with quarterly/monthly subset.

Three-way gender comparison composed from UN SDG 8.5.2 unemployment (DF_SDG_0852_SEX_AGE_RT), employment-to-population ratio (DF_EMP_DWAP), and labour force participation (DF_EAP_DWAP). All series are split by sex (Total, Male, Female) so that a consumer can derive male-female differentials directly.

Mean weekly hours actually worked from ILO dataflow DF_HOW_TEMP_SEX_AGE_ECO_NB. The ECO dimension selects economic activity (ISIC sector): default ECO_AGGREGATE_TOTAL returns the all-industry average. Use ECO_AGGREGATE_AGR, ECO_AGGREGATE_IND, ECO_AGGREGATE_MAN, ECO_AGGREGATE_SER, etc. for sector detail.

Share of non-agricultural employment in the informal sector from ILO dataflow DF_EMP_NIFL_SEX_INS_RT. Key dimension INS indexes institutional sector (INS_SECTOR_TOTAL, INS_SECTOR_PRI private, INS_SECTOR_PUB public, INS_SECTOR_FRM formal, INS_SECTOR_INF informal). Coverage is broadest for developing countries.

parameters.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (developing countries coverage).
parameters.name: countries

schema.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (developing countries coverage).
schema.title: Countries

endpoints.description: Occupational injury rates per 100,000 workers, combined from two ILO dataflows: DF_INJ_FATL_ECO_RT (fatal) and DF_INJ_NFTL_ECO_RT (non-fatal). Coverage depends on national OSH reporting; not all ILO members publish these.
Economically active population as a share of the working-age population, from ILO dataflow DF_EAP_DWAP_SEX_AGE_RT. Annual primary, 180+ countries.
Labour productivity measured as GDP per employed person (constant 2017 PPP US dollars), from ILO dataflow DF_LAP_2GDP_NOC_RT. Annual series from ILO modelled estimates; covers 180+ countries.

Labour dispute statistics from two ILO dataflows: DF_STR_TSTR_ECO_NB (number of strikes by economic activity) and DF_STR_DAYS_ECO_NB (working days lost). Reporting coverage is voluntary and varies by country.

Headline unemployment rate from ILO dataflow DF_UNE_DEAP_SEX_AGE_RT. Monthly/quarterly/annual depending on country reporting. Supports ISO-3 multi-country keys (USA+GBR+DEU), sex (SEX_T total, SEX_M male, SEX_F female), and age group (default 15+).

endpoints.description: arccos(close); range [0, π]; NaN for close outside [-1, 1].

Cumulative volume-flow indicator. Each bar adds Money Flow Volume = ((close-low) - (high-close)) / (high-low) × volume. Path-dependent and unbounded — interpret divergences against price rather than absolute level. `time_period` required by API contract but ignored (AD is cumulative).

parameters.description: Required by API contract; AD is cumulative.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; AD is cumulative.
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: EMA(AD, 3) - EMA(AD, 10) — Marc Chaikin's oscillator built on the Accumulation/Distribution Line. Bounded around zero, easier to read than raw AD. `time_period` ignored — Chaikin's fast=3 / slow=10 are baked in.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; ADOSC uses fixed Chaikin defaults (fast=3, slow=10).
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; ADOSC uses fixed Chaikin defaults (fast=3, slow=10).
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: Wilder's ADX. Measures trend strength only — does not say up or down. Above 25 = trending market, below 20 = ranging. Pair with PLUS_DI / MINUS_DI (separate routes) for direction. Default `time_period=14`.
Smoothed ADX = (ADX[t] + ADX[t-time_period]) / 2. Slower trend-strength signal than raw ADX.
Aroon up = bars since highest high, scaled 0-100; Aroon down = bars since lowest low, scaled 0-100. Strong uptrend when Aroon up is near 100 and Aroon down is near 0. Default `time_period=25`.
Aroon up - Aroon down. Range -100..+100. Positive = uptrend dominant. Default `time_period=25`.
arcsin(close); range [-π/2, π/2]; NaN for close outside [-1, 1].
arctan(close); range (-π/2, π/2); always defined.
Wilder's ATR. Smoothed measure of price range covering gaps and intra-bar volatility. Default `time_period=14`. Used widely for stop-loss sizing and as a building block for ATR Bands / Keltner.
Volatility envelope: middle = close, upper / lower = close ±2*ATR over `time_period`. Useful for trailing-stop placement and as a proxy for Bollinger Bands when std-dev is unstable.
(open + high + low + close) / 4. Per-bar OHLC composite. Same as /ohlc4 — exposed under both names for TA-Lib / TradingView parity.
Bollinger Bands — middle line is SMA(close, time_period), upper / lower are middle ±2 standard deviations. Each observation row contains all three lines so they're time-aligned for plotting.

Rolling beta of `symbol` vs `benchmark` (default SPY). BETA = cov(sym_returns, bench_returns) / var(bench_returns). Beta=1 = moves with benchmark, >1 = amplifies, <1 = damps, negative = inverse. Computed on log returns over `time_period` bars.
(close - open) / (high - low). Per-bar value, range typically -1..+1. `time_period` ignored.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; BOP is per-bar.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; BOP is per-bar.
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: Lambert's CCI. Mean-deviation oscillator centered at 0. Above +100 = strong uptrend / overbought, below -100 = strong downtrend / oversold. Default `time_period=20`.
Smallest integer >= close.
Choppiness Index, range 0-100. Below 38 = strong trending market, above 61 = choppy / ranging. Useful as a regime filter — disable trend-following signals when market is choppy. Default 14.
Rolling-window money-flow ratio: sum of money_flow_volume over `time_period` bars divided by sum of volume. Range -1..+1. Above 0 = accumulation pressure, below 0 = distribution. Default 20.
Symmetric momentum oscillator, range -100..+100. Like RSI but centered at 0.
Long-term buy signal indicator: WMA(time_period) of ROC(11)+ROC(14). Default time_period=10. Buy signal when curve crosses above 0 from negative.
Rolling Pearson correlation of `symbol` log-returns vs `benchmark` (default SPY) log-returns over `time_period` bars. Range -1..+1. Useful for diversification analysis and pairs-trading screen.
cos(close); close interpreted as radians.
cosh(close); inf overflow for close > ~700.
Composite oscillator averaging RSI(close,N) + RSI(streak,2) + PercentRank(ROC1,100). Range 0-100. Default time_period=3 per Connors original spec.
DEMA = 2*EMA - EMA(EMA). Reduces lag inherent in standard EMA by subtracting the lag of EMA-of-EMA.
Donchian Channel — upper = highest_high over window, lower = lowest_low, middle = (upper + lower) / 2. Classic breakout indicator (Turtle traders).
Removes long-term trend by subtracting a shifted SMA. Highlights short-term cycles. time_period=window (default 20).
Pre-ADX raw value: |+DI - -DI| / (+DI + -DI) * 100. ADX is just smoothed DX.
Exponential Moving Average over the close price. Reacts faster to recent price changes than SMA. Multi-period via comma-separated `time_period`.
exp(close); inf overflow for close > ~700.
Largest integer <= close.
Heikin-Ashi candle transform. Smoothed OHLC for trend visualization. Multi-line: ha_open/ha_high/ha_low/ha_close. time_period ignored (per-bar). Wave F.
(high + low + close) / 3 — TradingView naming for typical price. Aliased /typprice.
Ehlers' Hilbert Transform Discriminator output: smoothed dominant cycle period in bars. Bounded to [6, 50]. Used for adaptive indicator tuning — feed into MA period, oscillator window, etc.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; algorithm self-adaptive.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; algorithm self-adaptive.
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: Phase of the dominant cycle in degrees, derived from atan2(q, i). Range -180 to +180. Used for cycle-timing entries: enter long when phase crosses zero from negative to positive.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; algorithm self-adaptive.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: In-phase (i) and quadrature (q) components of the Hilbert Transform Discriminator. Vector representation of the dominant cycle: (i, q) traces an ellipse in clean cycles, scrambled in trends.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; algorithm self-adaptive.

endpoints.description: Ehlers' SineWave indicator: sine = sin(phase), lead_sine = sin(phase + 45°). lead_sine crosses sine at cycle turning points — useful as buy / sell signal in pure cycle markets. In trending markets, lines stay flat / parallel (signal is HT_TRENDMODE = 1).

parameters.description: Required by API contract; algorithm self-adaptive.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; algorithm self-adaptive.
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: John Ehlers' Hilbert Transform Trendline — adaptive moving average centered over the detected dominant cycle period. Smoother than fixed-window MAs in trending markets, faster to track turning points than constant-period EMAs. `time_period` is required by API contract but ignored — algorithm uses self-detected cycle period.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; HT_TRENDLINE is self-adaptive and ignores this value.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; HT_TRENDLINE is self-adaptive and ignores this value.
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: Binary regime classifier: 1 = trending, 0 = cycling. Heuristic based on dominant cycle period (long = trend) and SineWave separation (flat sine = trend). Use as filter: enable trend-following when 1, enable cycle / mean-reversion when 0.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; algorithm self-adaptive.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; algorithm self-adaptive.
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: Ichimoku Cloud — four lines per observation: conversion_line (Tenkan-sen, fast), base_line (Kijun-sen, medium), senkou_span_a (leading A, midpoint of Tenkan + Kijun), senkou_span_b (leading B, slow window midpoint). `time_period` controls Tenkan window only; Kijun (26) and Senkou-B (52) are fixed per Ichimoku methodology. Lagging span (Chikou) intentionally omitted — derive from close shifted by 26 periods.

KAMA — smoothing constant adapts to price efficiency ratio: trends faster (closer to fast EMA), ranges slower (closer to slow EMA). Defaults: fast=2, slow=30. `time_period` is the efficiency-ratio window.
Keltner Channel — middle = EMA(close), upper / lower = middle ± 2*ATR. Volatility-adaptive bands; tighter than Bollinger in low-vol regimes and wider in high-vol regimes.
Pring's KST momentum. Sum of 4 smoothed ROCs (10/15/20/30 with smoothings 10/10/10/15). 9-bar signal line. Multi-line: kst/signal/diff. time_period ignored (fixed windows).

parameters.description: Required by API contract; KST uses fixed Pring windows.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; KST uses fixed Pring windows.
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: Fitted endpoint of rolling linear regression over `time_period` close values.
Slope expressed as angle in degrees: arctan(slope) * 180/pi. Cross-instrument comparable trend strength.
Y-intercept of rolling linear regression. Combined with slope gives full line equation.
Slope of rolling linear regression. Positive = uptrend, negative = downtrend.
ln(close); NaN for close <= 0.
log10(close); NaN for close <= 0.
ln(close / close[t-n]). Standard quant return — additive across periods, near-normal distribution. Building block for Sharpe / Sortino / VaR.

MACD with Appel defaults (fast=12, slow=26, signal=9). Each observation row contains macd, signal, hist (= macd - signal) so the lines are time-aligned for plotting. `time_period` is required by API contract but ignored — MACD uses fixed Appel windows.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; MACD uses fixed Appel windows (12/26/9).
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; MACD uses fixed Appel windows (12/26/9).
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: Slope of the MACD line over `time_period` bars. MACD uses fixed Appel windows (12/26/9). time_period = look-back (default 5).

John Ehlers' MESA Adaptive MA. Detects the dominant cycle period via Hilbert Transform Discriminator and adjusts smoothing alpha to the phase change of that cycle. Returns two lines: MAMA (fast adaptive) and FAMA (Following Adaptive MA, slower). Crossovers between MAMA / FAMA generate signals. `time_period` is required by API contract but ignored — algorithm uses Ehlers defaults (fast_limit=0.5, slow_limit=0.05).

parameters.description: Required by API contract; MAMA is self-adaptive and ignores this value.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; MAMA is self-adaptive and ignores this value.
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: Rolling max of close over time_period bars. time_period required (default 14). Wave F.
Position (0..time_period-1) of max within rolling window. 0 = oldest bar in window. TA-Lib semantics. Wave F.
(high + low) / 2 — midpoint of intra-bar range, used as anchor for Donchian / Keltner.
Volume-weighted RSI. Range 0-100. Above 80 = overbought, below 20 = oversold. Best on liquid securities; thin volume distorts. Default `time_period=14`.
Midpoint = (highest_close + lowest_close) / 2 over a window. Centerline of close-price range.
Midprice = (highest_high + lowest_low) / 2 over a window. Centerline of high-low range, sensitive to wicks.
Rolling min of close over time_period bars. time_period required (default 14). Wave F.
Position (0..time_period-1) of min within rolling window. See /maxindex. Wave F.
Bear-trend strength component of ADX. Pair with /plus_di: -DI > +DI = downtrend.
Wilder-smoothed -DM raw component. Used internally by ADX; exposed separately for callers building custom directional systems.
Raw momentum: close - close[t-n]. Pre-rate-of-change form. Default `time_period=10`.
ATR expressed as a percentage of close: ATR / close * 100. Cross-instrument comparable, unlike raw ATR. Useful for portfolio-level volatility ranking.
Joe Granville's classic. Cumulative volume signed by close direction: +volume on up bars, -volume on down bars. Path-dependent and unbounded — interpret slope and divergences. `time_period` ignored.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; OBV is cumulative.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; OBV is cumulative.

endpoints.description: (open + high + low + close) / 4 — TradingView naming for average price. Aliased /avgprice.
(close - close[t-n]) / close[t-n]. Decimal form, same as ROCP. Used in return-distribution analysis.
(close - lower band) / (upper - lower) on Bollinger Bands. >1.0 = above upper; <0.0 = below lower. time_period=BB window (default 20), std=2.

Classic floor-trader pivot points. Each row contains pivot P plus three resistance levels (R1, R2, R3) and three support levels (S1, S2, S3). Levels for current bar are derived from prior bar's H/L/C — `time_period` controls lookback shift (default 1 = prior bar).

endpoints.description: Bull-trend strength component of ADX. Pair with /minus_di for direction signal: +DI > -DI = uptrend.
Wilder-smoothed +DM raw component. Used internally by ADX; exposed separately for callers building custom directional systems.
PPO is MACD expressed as a percentage of slow EMA, allowing comparison across instruments with different price levels. Appel defaults (fast=12, slow=26, signal=9). `time_period` ignored.

endpoints.description: close - close[t-time_period]. Same value as MOM but tagged statistic for portfolio / return-distribution use.
Percentage change of close over `time_period` bars: ((close - close[t-n]) / close[t-n]) * 100. Default 12.
Decimal form of ROC: (close - close[t-n]) / close[t-n]. Same as ROC / 100.
Multiplicative form: close / close[t-n]. Useful for compounding analysis.
(close / close.shift(time_period)) * 100. Same as /rocr but in percentage form. Default time_period=10.

Wilder's RSI. 0-100 oscillator measuring speed and magnitude of price changes. Above 70 commonly read as overbought, below 30 as oversold. Multi-period (`time_period=14,21,30`) returns separate series per period for divergence analysis.
Today's volume / rolling avg volume. >1.0 = unusually heavy day; <1.0 = light. time_period=lookback window (default 20). Wave F.
sin(close); close interpreted as radians.
sinh(close); inf overflow for close > ~700.

Simple Moving Average over the close price. Optionally accepts a comma-separated list of `time_period` values (max 5) to compute multiple SMAs in one request — useful for plotting fast / slow MA overlays without parallel calls.

endpoints.description: sqrt(close); NaN for close < 0.
Rolling sample standard deviation of close. Building block for Bollinger Bands.
Lane's Stochastic with %D smoothing fixed at 3 bars. Returns %K (main line) and %D (signal). Both 0-100. Above 80 = overbought, below 20 = oversold. `time_period` is the %K window.
Raw fast %K (no smoothing). Returns single line. For smoothed %K + %D pair, use /stoch instead.

Stochastic applied to RSI series — second-derivative momentum. Range 0-1 (note: smaller than %K's 0-100 because of the underlying RSI scale). Smoothing fixed at 3 (Lane convention). `time_period` is the RSI + Stoch window.
Direction-aware trailing stop based on ATR with multiplier=3.0. Returns the supertrend line: when below price the trend is up, when above price the trend is down. Flips on close-vs-band crossover.
T3 by Tim Tillson — 6-EMA cascade with weighted polynomial sum, volume factor v=0.7. Smooth, low-lag alternative to DEMA / TEMA.
tan(close); inf at close = π/2 + n*π.
tanh(close); range (-1, 1); always defined.
TEMA = 3*EMA - 3*EMA(EMA) + EMA(EMA(EMA)). Further reduces lag of DEMA. Smooth + responsive.
Per-bar True Range = max(H - L, |H - C[-1]|, |L - C[-1]|). Building block for ATR. `time_period` ignored — TRANGE is per-bar. For smoothed version use /atr.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; TRANGE is per-bar.
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; TRANGE is per-bar.
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: TRIMA = SMA(SMA(close, k), k) where k = ceil((time_period + 1) / 2). Smoother than SMA, double-smoothed. Matches TA-Lib convention.
1-day rate of change of triple-smoothed EMA. Slow oscillator, filters noise. Default 15.
Linear regression projected one bar ahead. Differs from LINEARREG (which is the last fitted point) by one slope-step.
Double-smoothed momentum. `time_period` is the fast window; slow is fixed at 25. Default fast=13.
(high + low + close) / 3. Used internally by CCI, MFI, VWAP. Same as /hlc3.
Peter Martin's Ulcer Index — RMS of percent drawdowns from rolling peak. Unlike ATR / stddev, asymmetric: only counts downside pain. Used in Ulcer Performance Index (return / UI). Default 14.
Williams' Ultimate Oscillator. Combines 3 lookback windows (7/14/28). `time_period` ignored.

parameters.description: Required by API contract; ULTOSC uses fixed Williams windows (7/14/28).
parameters.name: time_period

schema.description: Required by API contract; ULTOSC uses fixed Williams windows (7/14/28).
schema.title: Time Period

endpoints.description: Rolling sample variance of close. Squared standard deviation.
VWAP = sum(typical_price * volume) / sum(volume) over the window. Institutional benchmark for fair-price execution.
(high + low + 2*close) / 4. Doubles the close weight versus typical price; gives extra emphasis to close in trending markets.
Williams %R — inverted Stochastic, range -100..0. Above -20 = overbought, below -80 = oversold. Lookback `time_period` (default 14).
Weighted Moving Average — linearly weighted by recency. Less smooth than EMA, more responsive than SMA.
(close - SMA) / STDDEV. Number of standard deviations close is from rolling mean. Stationary substitute for raw price in ML features.
